spec.title: DLsite and Steam recorded storefront adapters

humanSummary.text: Re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers after recorded-shape parser, seed-target gating, and drift diagnostic repairs. Three non-blocking hardening findings were converted into follow-up DAG nodes.

findings.title: Unsupported storefront shape diagnostics are only representative
findings.description: Repair tests prove semantic diagnostics for representative unsupported shapes, but not every unsupported-shape case asserts full stepKey, sourceId, and sourceField metadata.

evidence.detail: Re-audit found unsupported-shape handling passes, but some cases rely on representative diagnostic metadata assertions.

findings.impact: A future parser drift branch could lose full diagnostic metadata for a less-common unsupported response shape without failing tests.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Every unsupported DLsite and Steam recorded-response shape covered by tests asserts fixtureId, sourceRevision, stepKey, sourceId, and sourceField.
Diagnostics remain CATALOG-012 semantic diagnostics rather than generic repository or validation errors.
Parser-only tests stay runnable without live network access.

proposedDagNode.title: Storefront unsupported-shape diagnostic coverage
proposedDagNode.summary: Tighten recorded DLsite and Steam unsupported-shape tests so every parse-drift case asserts full semantic diagnostic metadata.
deliverables: Fixture id, source revision, step key, source id, and source field assertions
acceptanceCriteria: Every unsupported DLsite and Steam recorded-response shape test asserts fixtureId, sourceRevision, stepKey, sourceId, and sourceField.
Unsupported storefront responses fail as CATALOG-012 semantic diagnostics, not generic repository or enum errors.
Parser-only diagnostic tests run without DATABASE_URL, live storefront access, or credentials.

findings.title: DLsite missing demand warning source fields use normalized paths
findings.description: DLsite missing demand warnings still report sourceField values such as demand.<field> even though the recorded-shaped fixture uses top-level source fields like wishlist_count.

evidence.detail: Re-audit found the warning is safe and non-blocking, but the sourceField path is not aligned with the recorded source response shape.

findings.impact: Reviewer-facing diagnostics are slightly less direct when comparing warnings to recorded DLsite responses.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: DLsite missing demand warnings use sourceField paths matching the recorded source response shape.
Tests assert warning sourceField values for missing dl_count, wishlist_count, rank, rating, and rating histogram fields.
Existing demand facts and parse-drift diagnostics remain unchanged except for clearer sourceField paths.

proposedDagNode.title: DLsite demand warning source-field alignment
proposedDagNode.summary: Align DLsite missing-demand warning sourceField paths with recorded source response fields rather than normalized demand paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Missing dl_count, wishlist_count, rank, rating, and rating histogram warnings cite recorded source response field paths.
Warning diagnostics still include fixtureId, sourceRevision, stepKey, and sourceId.
auditFocus: Diagnostics pointing at normalized fields instead of source fields

findings.title: Recorded storefront provenance kind clarity
findings.description: Recorded storefront imports still rely on generic crawler provenance behavior where fixture-mode evidence can be hard to distinguish from live raw-cache evidence.

evidence.detail: Initial audit found sourceRecordKind=recorded_fixture exists, while crawler provenance behavior can still present recorded fixture imports as raw cache provenance.

findings.impact: Benchmark and catalog explanations may be less clear about whether storefront facts came from public recorded fixtures or live raw cache captures.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Recorded storefront imports persist sourceRecordKind=recorded_fixture or expose an equivalent fixture-mode marker.
Fixture tests distinguish recorded fixture evidence from live/raw cache evidence.
Benchmark and opportunity explanations can identify recorded storefront evidence without implying live capture.

proposedDagNode.title: Recorded storefront provenance kind clarity
proposedDagNode.summary: Clarify recorded storefront importer provenance so fixture-backed catalog facts cannot be mistaken for live raw-cache evidence.
deliverables: Explanation fixture showing recorded storefront evidence
acceptanceCriteria: Recorded storefront imports persist sourceRecordKind=recorded_fixture or an equivalent fixture-mode provenance marker.
Tests distinguish recorded fixture provenance from live/raw cache provenance.
Catalog explanation output can identify recorded storefront evidence without claiming live collection.
auditFocus: Fixture evidence hidden as raw cache
